metadata.title: States PCS: Odisha GK & General Studies for OPSC and other Odisha Exams

questions.question: Hiuen Tsang visited Odisha in?
questions.explanation: Chinese traveler Hiuen Tsang visited Odisha in 638-39 AD. He describes Chelitalo as a busy port.

questions.question: Which dynasty marked the turning point of Odishan Coin?
questions.explanation: The gold coin of the Ganga dynasty was known as fanams. During the beginning of this dynasty Odishan coin witnessed a turning point.

questions.question: Which part of ancient Odisha often referred to as “ Land of Honey”?
questions.explanation: In Tamil literature, Kongu is known as Honey. So it is explained as the “Land of Honey”.

questions.question: Which of the following was a famous poet, also a contemporary to Rajaraja II?
questions.explanation: Jayadeva was a famous court poet of the ruler of Bengal Laxmansena. He was also contemporary to Rajaraja II. Gita Govind was written by Jayadeva.

questions.question: Who abolished the Chaukidari Tax System?
questions.explanation: Purusottomadeva abolished the Chaukidari Tax system which was previously imposed on the Brahmins.

questions.question: In the year 1849 which part of Odisha came under the direct control of British by the application of Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: The doctrine of Lapse was introduced by Lord Dalhousie. In the year 1848, it brought Satara (Maharashtra) as the first place under the direct control of the British. Sambalpur of Odisha faced the same in the year 1849.

questions.question: Chandan Hajuri was associated with which movement of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Chandan Hajuri or Chakhi Khuntia played a significant role in the Sepoy mutiny, 1857.

questions.question: Famous newspaper ‘The Samaja” Started in which year?
questions.explanation: The Odiya daily newspaper “The Samaja” published in the year 1919. It is managed by Servants of the People Society (Lok Sevak Mondal).

questions.question: Which among the following is/ are the effects of the Prajamandal movement?a) People realized the significance of Non-violenceb) Accelerated the process of merging native princely states into Odishac) British rule was eradicated from Odishad) Ended the monarchy in OdishaSelect the correct option from the codes given below
questions.explanation: Consequences of Prajamandal Movements are- People realized the significance of Non-violence, Accelerated the process of merging native princely states into Odisha, Ended the monarchy in Odisha, King became tolerant towards people.

questions.question: Inside which jail Gopabandhu Das wrote the famous novel “Bandira Atmakatha”?
questions.explanation: Bandira Atmakatha (The biography of a Prisoner) was written by Gopabandhu Das when he was imprisoned in Hazaribag Jail. The novel reflects his immense love towards the people of Odisha.
